rationale: Targets the large-data/performance axis (train case perf-large-list grades virtualization/pagination, stable keys, and memoization) which the Phase 4 rubric never covered, so the agent had no self-check prompting it. It generalizes because virtualization + stable keys + memoization is a reusable rule for any data-heavy UI (tables, feeds, search results), not a memorized fact about the train case, and it's orthogonal to the heldout concerns (tokens, boundary, security, i18n) so it shouldn't regress them.
reason: no real gain: train -0.0107 (need >= eps_train 0.0484) / held-out -0.0455

rationale: Targets the cross-cutting "verification reported?" rubric weakness that recurs in both splits (token/responsive/long-content/perf/a11y on train; tablet/self-review/i18n on held-out). The runner has tools.mode=none, so Phase 3's tool-conditional phrasing ("가능하면 Bash로…", "스크린샷 수단이 있으면…") lets the agent skip verification; appending "도구가 없어도 검증을 생략하지 말고, 각 확인 항목의 결과를 구체적으로 짚어 보고한다" closes that loophole and demands concrete per-check reporting, generalizing to any case rather than memorizing specifics.
reason: no real gain: train -0.0285 (need >= eps_train 0.0484) / held-out +0.0000

rationale: Targets the Accessibility rubric's dismissable-overlay axis (focus restoration on close, Esc dismiss, role/aria-modal/aria-expanded) — the prompt currently has zero coverage of it (verified: role/dialog/오버레이/포커스 복귀 = 0 occurrences), yet two train cases (modal-state-scope, icon-button-a11y) grade exactly this knowledge their inputs withhold. It generalizes as a reusable rule for any overlay/disclosure (modal, dropdown, popover, drawer, menu) and aligns with held-out tablet-breakpoint-nav (aria-expanded), rather than memorizing a single case; it does not repeat the failure log's performance or verification-reporting ideas.
reason: no real gain: train -0.0179 (need >= eps_train 0.0484) / held-out -0.0455

rationale: Targets the State & Robustness rubric: the prompt covers focus + DATA states (loading/error/empty) but never interactive-control states (hover/disabled) or async-action robustness (disable-while-submitting to block double-submit + action loading). Generalizes because every interactive control/async action across cases needs these, directly flipping design-fidelity-states' disabled/loading items and the held-out self-review's planted disable-on-submit flaw.
reason: no real gain: train +0.0000 (need >= eps_train 0.0484) / held-out -0.0455

rationale: Targets the Grounding & Reuse rubric: Phase 0's inventory step is component-only ("컴포넌트 재고", listing just Button/Input/Modal), so the runner can inline-reinvent existing hooks/utils — exactly the gap the train case `reuse-existing-hook-util` grades (reusing useDebounce/formatCurrency + showing a pre-code hook/util inventory check). Extending the scan to 훅·유틸·헬퍼 generalizes the no-reinvent principle to any reusable unit rather than memorizing specific names, and is distinct from all 4 failure-log items (perf, verification-reporting, overlay focus, data-states).
reason: no real gain: train +0.0179 (need >= eps_train 0.0484) / held-out +0.0227

rationale: Targets the Responsive/visual-overflow rubric weakness: the prompt only treats overflow as a Phase 3 verify concern, never as a build/plan rule, so the long-content-overflow train case (grades surviving long names + assuming edge data, not happy-path-only) leaks. The added Phase 1 clause (truncate/wrap/min-width:0 for extreme content) generalizes to any dynamic text/number and also covers held-out i18n text-expansion and tablet-overflow items, so it should lift train without regressing held-out.
